_source_file: 2026-01-01 - North Shore Ice Arena January 2026 General Ledger.pdf

extraction_notes: This is a full general ledger for January 2026 (accrual basis), not a summarized P&L or budget document. Revenue and expense figures represent January 2026 activity (period movements) only, not year-to-date cumulative totals, though account balances shown are cumulative since fiscal year start (July 1, 2025). Key mapping decisions: ice_rental mapped to Contract ice sales ($152,186.92); lessons_programs mapped to Public programs + Adult programs ($52,204.07 + $28,050.00 youth + $8,100.00 men's league = $88,354.07) plus on-ice/off-ice instruction revenue components embedded in Counter Sales invoice 2819; food_beverage mapped to JJ Cafe concession lease ($1,000.00); other revenue includes GVN Lease ($4,258.00), Locker Room Lease ($1,000.00), Advertising revenue ($1,700.00), CC fees collected ($377.25), Interest Income ($2,514.70), net of Change in MV Net Assets Loss (-$954.19) and Club Sports Consulting Group second billing ($15,499.14) which appears to be a mid-month management/payroll advance rather than a separate management fee — it is included in other expenses. The $15,499.14 CSCG mid-month payment is categorized under other expenses as it appears to be a payroll funding transfer rather than a separately categorized management fee. Land lease to Techny ($32,291.67) plus Techny loan interest ($1,174.00) included in other expenses. Net income is approximate and derived from January period activity only. Payroll figures are sourced entirely from Club Sports Consulting Group bills per the GL structure. No depreciation or amortization journal entries were posted in January per the GL. The GL is 18 pages and appears complete for the month.
_source_file: 2026-01-31 - North Shore Ice Arena January General Ledger.pdf

extraction_notes: Document contains multiple financial statements: (1) January 2026 P&L actual (single month), (2) YTD P&L actual July 2025 - January 2026, (3) Budget vs Actuals comparison for January 2026 and YTD, (4) Balance Sheet as of January 31 2026, (5) Cash Forecast through April 2026, (6) YTD P&L comparison vs prior year (Jan 2025). Revenue and expense fields in the primary JSON reflect January 2026 actuals only. Ice rental mapped to Contract ice sales only; lessons_programs includes Public programs (Rink Rentals + Youth programs) + Adult programs (Men's League); food_beverage mapped to Lease Restaurant/Bar; other revenue includes Advertising, CC/Bank Fees, Lease Locker Room, GVN Lease. Expense 'other' includes Land Lease (32,291.67), Interest on Techny Loan (1,174.00), Contract Services (115.00), Equipment Expenses (1,404.22), Trustee Admin (500.00), and Office Expenses (1,999.55). Debt service shows $0 for January as no bond payment was recorded in the monthly P&L; YTD Interest Expense - DSRF of $188,205.35 is present in YTD figures. No pro shop revenue identified. Document is complete and internally consistent.
_source_file: 2026-01-31 - North Shore Ice Arena January 2026 Financial Overview.pdf

extraction_notes: Document contains three columns: Fiscal 2026 Budget Proposal (primary), Fiscal 2025 actuals, and Fiscal 2024 actuals. All three years extracted verbatim. Revenue and expense mappings to standard schema fields are approximations: ice_rental mapped from Contract Ice Sales + Tournaments ($1,414,567 + $38,292 = $1,452,859); food_beverage mapped from Restaurant/Bar lease component of Lease Income ($24,000); lessons_programs mapped from Public Programs and Ice Sales ($821,834); other revenue includes remaining Lease Income minus restaurant component ($84,896 - $24,000 = $60,896) plus Other Building Revenue ($19,500) = $80,396 — note minor rounding; expense schema fields are approximations as document uses different categorization. Utilities estimated from Operations detail (Electric $186,000 + Gas $27,600 + Water/Sewer $21,000 + Propane $4,080 + Cable/Internet $13,392 - approximately $238,992 but not all items cleanly map). Net income reported as Cash Available after Debt Services ($36,086) which is after land lease and debt service. Net Operating Income before land lease and debt service is $1,123,783. Monthly detail provided for all line items across all pages. Page 3 contract ice detail shows only 10 of 12 months populated with named customers; July and August appear blank in the monthly breakdown but totals reconcile. Debt service interest breakdown shows $188,178 in August and $180,340 in October only — remaining months blank suggesting interest is accrued or paid in lump sums. Capital Expenditures in Operations show only two months populated ($8,195 + $5,800 = $13,995) which differs from the sum notation; totals row for Operations reconciles to $370,968. Techny Property Fee appears only in July Office column ($3,100) with no annual total shown separately. Interest on Deferred Rent shows $2,629 in July and $2,628.96 in June. Document appears complete for a budget proposal; no pages appear missing.
_source_file: 2026-06-30 - Fiscal 2026 Budget Proposal for Ice Facility.pdf

extraction_notes: Document is the Fiscal Year 2026 (July 1 2025 - June 30 2026) Budget Proposal prepared by CSCG for NSIA. All primary revenue and expense line items extracted from Page 1 summary and supported by detailed monthly schedules on subsequent pages. Fiscal 2025 and Fiscal 2024 actuals are also present in the document for comparison but are not the primary budget figures. Net income of $36,086 represents Cash Available after Debt Services (Net Operating Income $1,123,783 less Land Lease $464,179 less Debt Service $623,518). The total expenses figure of $1,255,307 excludes Land Lease and Debt Service which are shown as below-the-line items. Revenue categorization note: ice_rental mapped to Contract Ice Sales + Tournaments ($1,414,567 + $38,292 = $1,452,859); lessons_programs mapped to Youth Camp ($155,625); skating_admission mapped to Ice Sales within Public Programs ($642,809); other mapped to Men's League revenue + Other Building Revenue + Lease Income ($23,400 + $19,500 + $84,896 = $127,796 rounded to $128,571 including rounding from totals). Monthly detail tables for payroll expense by month appear blank/unpopulated in the extracted text for some sections but annual totals are confirmed on Page 1 summary. Land Lease total of $464,179 includes new loan components (loan payback $62,500, interest on loan $12,800, deferred rent interest $2,629) not present in prior years. Repair and Replacement shows negative ($-15,137) for Fiscal 2024 actual which is noted as unusual.

Full audited financial statements for the year ended June 30, 2025, audited by Miller Verchota, Inc., CPA, Crystal Lake, Illinois, issued October 1, 2025. All major statements extracted: Statement of Financial Position, Statement of Activities, Statement of Functional Expenses, Statement of Cash Flows, and Notes. Revenue mapping notes: 'Contracted rental fees' mapped to ice_rental; 'Rentals for public programs' mapped to skating_admission (closest standard category — includes public session and program rentals); 'Tournaments' ($23,175), 'League fees' ($45,758), 'Facility rental' ($69,316), 'Interest' ($46,506), 'Contributions' ($290,919), 'Loss on disposal of assets' (-$165,820), and 'Unrealized loss on investments' (-$7,945) combined into revenue 'other' ($308,582). No pro_shop, food_beverage, or lessons_programs line items appear in the document. Expense mapping notes: debt_service includes interest expense - bond ($382,374) + interest expense - debt costs ($26,797) + interest expense - finance lease ($319,150) = $728,321; maintenance mapped to building and ground expenses ($93,140); 'other' expenses include amortization of right-of-use asset ($303,210), association fees ($3,043), audit/bookkeeping/legal ($33,286), bank and credit card fees ($18,018), bond trustee expense ($11,856), building operation expense ($112,229), depreciation ($221,548), real estate taxes ($133,309), software ($10,680), and youth program ($116,446) = $963,625. Total revenue per Statement of Activities is $2,418,509; disaggregated revenue table in Note 2 shows $2,545,768 which includes gross figures before losses — the $2,418,509 net figure is used as the authoritative total. Document is complete and all pages appear present.
_source_file: 2025-06-30 - North Shore Ice Arena Audited Financial Statements.pdf

extraction_notes: This document is an Audit Representation Letter dated October 1, 2025, submitted to Miller Verchota, Inc. for the audit of North Shore Ice Arena, LLC financial statements for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025. It contains no financial figures, line items, revenue, or expense data whatsoever. It is a standard management representation letter confirming responsibilities, information access, fraud disclosures, compliance, and tax-exempt status under IRC Section 501(c)(3). All numeric fields are set to 0.00 due to complete absence of financial data in this document. The actual audited financial statements (statement of financial position, statement of activities, statement of functional expenses, and statement of cash flows) are referenced but not included in this filing.
_source_file: 2025-06-30 - North Shore Ice Arena Financial Audit Representation Letter.pdf

extraction_notes: This document contains only an e-signature audit trail for NSIA Form 8879-TE, prepared by Miller Verchota, Inc. CPAs and e-signed by Pete Cecil on 10/10/2025. No financial line items, revenue, expense, or income figures are present in the provided pages. The actual financial statements or audit report content (Form 8879-TE and associated schedules) were not included in the extracted text. All financial fields are set to 0.00 due to absence of data. The fiscal year period cannot be determined from this document alone, though given NSIA's July 1–June 30 fiscal year and a submission date of October 10, 2025, the audit likely pertains to FY ending June 30, 2025 — but this is unconfirmed. Full financial data extraction requires the underlying audit report and financial statements.

This is a Form 990 tax return for fiscal year July 1, 2024 through June 30, 2025, submitted October 15, 2025. Document is complete and covers the full filing including Schedules A, D, K, O, and R. Revenue breakdown is not itemized by NSIA operational category (ice rental, pro shop, food & beverage, etc.) in the 990 — all program revenue is reported as a single line 'ICE ARENA PROGRAM REVENUE' of $2,185,533. Contributions of $290,919 represent grants received from related organizations (Wilmette Hockey Association $145,459 and Winnetka Hockey Club $145,460 per Schedule R). Investment income of $46,506 and net rental income of $69,316 are separately disclosed; asset sale loss of -$165,820 nets against gross proceeds of $107,429 vs. basis of $273,249. The 'other' revenue bucket in the structured output captures investment income ($46,506) plus net rental income ($69,316) plus net asset sale loss (-$165,820) = -$49,998, but total revenue per return is $2,426,454 reconciling as: $290,919 + $2,185,533 + $46,506 + $69,316 + (-$165,820) = $2,426,454. Payroll is reported as $0 on lines 5-10 of Part IX (no employees on W-3 per Part V line 2a = 0); all staffing is provided through Club Sports Management Group Inc (independent contractor) compensated $449,863 per Part VII Section B, captured within fees for services - other ($378,598 program + management components per Schedule O). Management fee line of $42,000 is a separate line item (Part IX line 11a). Occupancy of $885,570 likely includes facility lease/bond costs. Finance lease liability of $15,898,414 and tax-exempt bond liability of $5,702,800 represent significant long-term obligations. Organization carries negative net assets of -$2,939,532 at year end. The 'other' revenue field in the structured output above ($240,822) represents investment income ($46,506) + net rental income ($69,316) + net asset sale loss (-$165,820) + contributions/grants ($290,919) = $240,921 — minor rounding; contributions are included in 'other' since no grants/contributions category exists in the schema. Note: the schema 'other' for revenue was set to $240,822 = $2,426,454 total minus $2,185,533 program revenue. Expense 'other' field of $1,970,399 captures all expense lines not individually mapped to schema categories (occupancy $885,570 + depreciation $524,758 + fees-other $378,598 + legal $18,076 + accounting $15,210 + IT $10,680 + youth program $116,446 + bank/credit card fees $18,018 + association fees $3,043 = $1,970,399). Prior year figures also available in document for comparative purposes.
_source_file: 2025-10-15 - North Shore Ice Arena LLC Tax Exempt Form 990.pdf
